2605.22608.title: Agentic CLEAR: Automating Multi-Level Evaluation of LLM Agents
2605.22608.paper_summary: Agentic systems are becoming more capable: agents define strategies, take actions, and interact with different environments. This autonomy poses serious challenges for overseeing and assessing agent behavior. Most current tools are limited, focusing on observability with basic evaluation capabilities or imposing static, hand-crafted error taxonomies that cannot adapt to new domains. To address this gap, we present Agentic CLEAR, an automatic, dynamic, and easy-to-use evaluation framework. It produces textual insights into the agent behavior on three levels of granularity: system, trace, and node. Agentic CLEAR operates above the observability layer, enabling seamless integration and featuring an intuitive UI that makes agent evaluation highly accessible. In our experiments on four benchmarks, seven agentic settings, and tens of thousands of LLM calls, we show that Agentic CLEAR produces high-quality, data-driven, insightful feedback. Our analysis shows strong alignment with human-annotated errors and the ability to predict task success rate.
2605.22608.paper_summary_zh: 代理系統的能力正在增強：代理人定義策略、採取行動並與不同環境互動。這種自主性對監督和評估代理行為提出了嚴峻的挑戰。當前的大多數工具都有限，專注於可觀察性，具備基本的評估能力，或施加靜態的手工錯誤分類，無法適應新領域。為了解決這一空白，我們提出了Agentic CLEAR，一個自動化、動態且易於使用的評估框架。它在三個粒度層次上提供代理行為的文本洞察：系統、追蹤和節點。Agentic CLEAR運行在可觀察性層之上，實現無縫集成，並具備直觀的用戶界面，使代理評估變得非常便捷。在我們對四個基準、七個代理設置和數萬次LLM調用的實驗中，我們顯示Agentic CLEAR產生高質量、數據驅動的深刻反饋。我們的分析顯示出與人類標註錯誤的強烈一致性以及預測任務成功率的能力。
2605.22608.author: Asaf Yehudai et.al.
2605.22608.authors: Asaf Yehudai, Lilach Eden, Michal Shmueli-Scheuer
